**CP00-198 — PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** (does not block pack or goal closeout)

- **P0/P1/P2: none.** **P3: 3** (all non-blocking maintainability items)
  - `CP198-R01` — dedicated `rp06:dms-core:validate` gate not in the closeout command set (contract still covered transitively via `npm test`).
  - `CP198-R02` — contract JSON embeds a descriptor/fixture/model snapshot not deep-compared to code-generated output (drift risk).
  - `CP198-R03` — `validators.js:65` hardcodes the `source_policy` allow-list instead of reusing exported `DMS_SOURCE_POLICIES` (values match today).

All four boundaries the prompt asked me to verify hold: descriptor-only no-write/no-runtime, synthetic-only data, Claude-as-read-only-reviewer (not final approver), and Hermes-as-deterministic-metadata-only. Independent read-only inspection confirms: (1) Coverage integrity — validateDmsCoreCp198Coverage enforces exactly 150 RP06 units spanning RP06.P00.M00.S01..RP06.P01.M02.S08 with the declared deliverable distribution (implementation 108, contract 8, security_audit 16, hermes_evidence 7, claude_review 3, ui 8) and required unit titles. (2) No-write/no-runtime boundary — no writeFile/appendFile/child_process/spawn/execSync/fetch/http/object-storage calls exist under packages/dms/src; every record and result object carries all-false no-write flags (writes_product_state, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, reads/writes_object_storage, executes_ocr/search_indexing, exposes_document_bytes/extracted_text), and validateDmsCoreRecord/validateDmsCoreCp198Foundation assert these closed. (3) No-real-data boundary — fixtures are synthetic-only (byte_size 0, sha256 all-zero, reserved storage pointer) with no_real_data/synthetic_only attestations. (4) Authority boundary intact — the Claude review packet is read_only:true with allowed_tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob, promotes_claude_to_final_approval and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation are asserted false, and the manifest contains zero production_ready:true over-claims (only the guardrail enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation:true). (5) Hermes is used solely as deterministic descriptor metadata and a boolean evidence builder, not embedded product runtime. Final closeout and the enterprise-trust claim still require the downstream Hermes receipt validation, adjudication, construction inspection, and human approval — consistent with the stated authority boundary.","findings":[{"id":"CP198-R01","severity":"P3","title":"Dedicated rp06:dms-core:validate contract gate is not wired into the closeout validation command set","file":"artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-198/review-baseline.json","line":1547,"details":"The mandated closeout validation_commands list (review-baseline.json lines 1547-1559) includes npm test, npm run validate, and the RP05/RP00/plan/weighted/fullplan/goal gates, but does not include the dedicated deterministic contract validator npm run rp06:dms-core:validate, which exists in package.json line 55 (node scripts/validate-rp06-dms-core-contract.mjs). The DMS contract is still exercised indirectly via npm test (packages/dms/test/model.test.js imports contracts/dms-core-contract.json), so coverage is not lost, but the standalone validator that explicitly checks schema_version, model_count 10, production_ready_candidate, and the CP00-199 handoff is not part of the gating command set.","recommendation":"Add npm run rp06:dms-core:validate to the closeout validation command matrix so the dedicated RP06 DMS Core contract validator runs as an explicit closeout gate rather than relying solely on transitive coverage through npm test.","post_review_disposition":"non_blocking_recommendation_for_owner_followup"},{"id":"CP198-R02","severity":"P3","title":"Contract JSON embeds a foundation_descriptor/fixture/model_definitions snapshot that is not deep-compared against code-generated output","file":"contracts/dms-core-contract.json","line":427,"details":"contracts/dms-core-contract.json embeds static snapshots: model_definitions (line 85), foundation_descriptor (line 427), and a fixture block (line 515). The test suite and scripts/validate-rp06-dms-core-contract.mjs assert specific scalar fields and counts (e.g. model_count 10, schema_version, production_ready_candidate, handoff target) rather than performing a structural deep-equality between the embedded JSON snapshot and the live createDmsCoreFoundationDescriptor()/createDmsCoreSyntheticFixture() output. A future change to the descriptor/fixture shape in service.js or registry.js could therefore drift from the committed JSON snapshot while still passing, as long as the individually asserted fields remain correct.","recommendation":"Add a test that deep-compares the embedded contract foundation_descriptor/fixture/model_definitions against the code-generated descriptor (normalizing volatile fields), so snapshot drift between the JSON contract and the source builders is caught deterministically.","post_review_disposition":"non_blocking_recommendation_for_owner_followup"},{"id":"CP198-R03","severity":"P3","title":"validators.js hardcodes the source_policy allow-list instead of reusing the exported DMS_SOURCE_POLICIES constant","file":"packages/dms/src/validators.js","line":65,"details":"validateDmsCoreRecord at packages/dms/src/validators.js line 65 inlines the source_policy allow-list as a literal ["source_required", "uncited_internal_note", "reserved_for_rp07_search"] rather than importing DMS_SOURCE_POLICIES, which is exported from registry.js line 68 with the identical values and is already reused by model.js (lines 146 and 160 via assertAllowed). The values match today so there is no current correctness divergence, but the duplicated literal is a drift risk: a future edit to DMS_SOURCE_POLICIES in the registry would silently fail to propagate to the validator.","recommendation":"Import DMS_SOURCE_POLICIES into validators.js and reference it in validateDmsCoreRecord so the source_policy allow-list has a single source of truth shared with the model factories.","post_review_disposition":"non_blocking_recommendation_for_owner_followup"}],"finding_counts":{"p0":0,"p1":0,"p2":0,"p3":3,"reported_p0":0,"reported_p1":0,"reported_p2":0,"reported_p3":3}},"terminal_reason":"completed","fast_mode_state":"off","uuid":"96417cc6-fc40-48b2-a0c1-ff2f32d1902e"}
